---
title: "Evidence"
subtitle: "The runnable backing for every claim in these docs."
jupyter: python3
sidebar: false
---

When a tutorial says `neuroim` catches a mistake that raw
`nibabel` + `numpy` would let through, this page is where that claim is
checked. Each row is one neuroimaging task implemented both ways under
[`examples/scenarios/`](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/tree/main/examples/scenarios),
with a `pytest` file that runs the comparison.

You don't need to read these to use the library — they exist so the
claims in the tutorials are verifiable rather than asserted. Every
public feature has a scenario, and the table below is regenerated from
the scenario reports each time the docs are built, so it stays in step
with the code.

| # | Scenario | Verdict | Report |
|---|----------|---------|--------|
| 01 | Scenario 01 — MNI Spotlight | Win on read-time, provenance, and total line count after the PAIN-1 and PAIN-3 fixes. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/01_mni_spotlight/REPORT.md) |
| 02 | Scenario 02 — ROI mean time series | Win on contract semantics; net wash on lines. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/02_roi_mean_timeseries/REPORT.md) |
| 02 | Scenario 02 — Seed-Sphere Mean (RENUMBERED to 03) | — | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/02_seed_sphere_mean/REPORT.md) |
| 03 | Scenario 03 — Seed-Sphere Mean | Clean win on every axis | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/03_seed_sphere_mean/REPORT.md) |
| 04 | Scenario 04 -- Temporal SNR Map | Clean win on every axis (after S04 PAIN-1/PAIN-2 landed). | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/04_temporal_snr_map/REPORT.md) |
| 05 | Scenario 05 — receipts across the IO boundary | Originally falsified the mission claim that ME-9 alone is sufficient; now fixed and guarded. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/05_receipt_io_boundary/REPORT.md) |
| 06 | Scenario 06 Report: Public Seed-To-Voxel Correlation | This is a credibility-gate win rather than a bespoke toy win. The computation is the same correlation math as the baseline, but neuroim makes the spatial con… | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/06_public_seed_correlation/REPORT.md) |
| 07 | Scenario 07 — Multi-subject concat | Win on contract semantics; an ME-9 chain-prefix gap surfaced adjacent to the scenario was fixed in the same iteration. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/07_multi_subject_concat/REPORT.md) |
| 08 | Scenario 08 — Pickle / multiprocessing handoff | Win on inspectability; neutral on numeric work. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/08_pickle_multiprocessing_handoff/REPORT.md) |
| 09 | Scenario 09 — Native-to-template provenance | Originally falsified inspectability for a careful-user workflow; now fixed and guarded. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/09_native_to_template_provenance/REPORT.md) |
| 10 | Scenario 10 — Searchlight Space Mismatch | Falsifies a mission-bearing safety claim. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/10_searchlight_space_mismatch/REPORT.md) |
| 11 | Scenario 11 -- Atlas-based per-parcel mean BOLD time series | After PAIN-1/3: neuroim now wins the call-site shape for this workflow. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/11_atlas_parcel_timeseries/REPORT.md) |
| 12 | Scenario 12 — File-Backed Affine Drift | Originally falsified a mission-bearing backend-safety claim; now fixed. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/12_file_backed_affine_drift/REPORT.md) |
| 12 | Scenario 12 -- Gaussian spatial smoothing at FWHM = 6 mm | neuroim now wins the call-site shape for this workflow | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/12_gaussian_smoothing/REPORT.md) |
| 13 | Scenario 13 -- Cited Nilearn port: seed-to-voxel correlation | The cited part is the value here, not the call-site delta. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/13_cited_nilearn_port/REPORT.md) |
| 13 | Scenario 13 — Pipeline Provenance Audit | Win on forensic inspectability. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/13_provenance_audit/REPORT.md) |
| 14 | Scenario 14 — Time-axis slicing as silent provenance loss | Falsifies "Receipts by default" at the slicing surface. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/14_temporal_slice_provenance/REPORT.md) |
| 15 | Scenario 15 - Block Parcel Contrast | Clear win on analysis readability and spatial safety. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/15_block_parcel_contrast/REPORT.md) |
| 16 | Scenario 16 - Smoothed Temporal-SNR QC Map | Safety, readability, and inspectability win. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/16_smoothed_tsnr_qc/REPORT.md) |
| 17 | Scenario 17 - Local Block-Effect Searchlight | Typed-result and spatial-safety win. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/17_searchlight_block_effect/REPORT.md) |
| 18 | Scenario 18 -- Cluster-table from a thresholded stat map | neuroim now wins the call-site shape for this workflow. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/18_cluster_table_post_stats/REPORT.md) |
| 19 | Scenario 19 — Group-mean tSNR across subjects in template space | Per-subject win, group-reduce gap. | [REPORT](https://github.com/bbuchsbaum/neuroim-python/blob/main/examples/scenarios/19_group_mean_template_tsnr/REPORT.md) |

*22 scenarios. Regenerated from `examples/scenarios/*/REPORT.md` at render time.*


## How to read a verdict

A scenario is a **win** if the `neuroim` form improves on at least one
of *read-time*, *safety*, or *inspectability* and ties or improves on
the rest. Surfaced pain points are filed as `PAIN-N` in the scenario's
`REPORT.md` even when the scenario wins overall — they are how the
suite stays honest. A verdict that says it *falsifies* a mission claim
is not a failure of the docs; it is the suite catching the library and
is tracked to a closing fix.

## Run them

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src:tests:. python -m pytest examples/scenarios -q
```

The shared fixture is
`tests/fixtures/realistic_bold.py::make_realistic_bold()` — a
deterministic 32×32×24×40 BOLD. Two tiny fixtures
(`golden_tests/fixtures/tiny_*.nii.gz`) ship for the doc snippets and
exercise the same code paths.

- [Start](../start/01-image.qmd) — the on-ramp whose every "Refuses to" block links back into this table.
